article.story:
Daniel Muñoz helped Colombia break through Congo DR's stingy defense and advance to the knockout stage of the World Cup with a 1-0 win on Tuesday night.

Muñoz scored in the 76th minute after the Colombians had been held back by a strong performance from Congo DR goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi, who made five difficult saves in the first 20 minutes.

Mpasi had no chance to stop Muñoz's left-footed strike from inside the area after the ball deflected off a defender.

Congo DR had opened its first World Cup in 52 years with a surprising 1-1 draw against Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal. Colombia, meanwhile, had opened with a 3-1 win over Uzbekistan.

Colombia have six points from two matches in Group K, two ahead of Portugal and five clear of Congo DR. Portugal routed Uzbekistan 5-0 on the back of a Ronaldo brace earlier Tuesday in Houston.

Now, Colombia needs a draw against Portugal on Saturday to win the group. That result would also be enough to send Portugal to the knockout stage.
